title: workflow-config
description: Schema for dso-config — configures commands and stack for the Digital Service Orchestra plugin. Note: the primary config format is now .claude/dso-config.conf (flat KEY=VALUE). This JSON schema documents the logical key structure and types. For the flat format, use validate-config.sh.

version.description: Config schema version (semver). Current supported version: 1.0.0

stack.description: Override auto-detected project stack. When omitted, detect-stack.sh infers from marker files.

paths.description: Project directory structure. Controls where hooks, scripts, and skills look for source code and tests.

app_dir.description: Application directory, relative to the repo root.

src_dir.description: Source code directory, relative to paths.app_dir.

test_unit_dir.description: Unit test directory, relative to paths.app_dir. Falls back to paths.test_dir when absent.

python_venv.description: Path to the Python virtual environment interpreter, relative to the repo root.

format.description: Auto-format hook configuration. Controls which files are processed by the auto-format PostToolUse hook.

extensions.description: File extensions to process (e.g. ['.py', '.ts']). Defaults to ['.py'] when absent.

source_dirs.description: Directories to restrict processing to. Paths relative to repo root or absolute. Defaults to ['app/src', 'app/tests'] when absent.

ci.description: CI polling configuration for ci-status.sh --wait adaptive backoff.

fast_gate_job.description: Display name of the fast-gate CI job (checked first on any failure for early exit). Defaults to 'Fast Gate'.

fast_fail_job.description: Display name of the job whose timeout-minutes defines the end of the fast-fail polling phase. Defaults to the fast_gate_job value.

test_ceil_job.description: Display name of the job whose timeout-minutes defines the end of the test polling phase. Defaults to 'Unit Tests'.

integration_workflow.description: GitHub Actions workflow name to check for integration test status. Used by validate-work and CI status checks to poll the integration test workflow separately from the main CI workflow. When absent, integration workflow status checks are skipped.

workflow_name.description: GitHub Actions workflow name for gh workflow run. Used by merge-to-main.sh for post-push CI trigger recovery. Preferred over the deprecated merge.ci_workflow_name. When absent, post-push CI trigger recovery is skipped.

staging.description: Staging environment configuration. Used by validate-work and staging sub-agents to run deploy checks, smoke tests, and route health verification against a deployed staging environment. All keys are optional — when absent, the corresponding check is skipped with a warning.

url.description: Base URL of the staging environment (e.g. 'https://staging.example.com'). Used by staging sub-agents as the target for health checks and smoke tests.

deploy_check.description: Path to a script or prompt file for checking deploy status, relative to the repo root. File extension determines how it is used: .sh = executed as a shell script; .md = read as a prompt for the staging sub-agent. Exit code contract for scripts: 0 = healthy (deploy complete and serving traffic), 1 = unhealthy (deploy failed or environment is down), 2 = deploying (deploy is still in progress, retry later).

test.description: Path to a script or prompt file for running smoke or acceptance tests against the staging environment, relative to the repo root. File extension determines how it is used: .sh = executed as a shell script (exit 0 = all tests passed, non-zero = one or more tests failed); .md = read as a prompt for the staging sub-agent.

routes.description: Comma-separated list of URL paths to health-check on the staging URL (e.g. '/,/upload,/history'). The orchestrator splits on commas at read time — this convention avoids YAML array syntax, keeping the value readable as a scalar via read-config.sh. Default: '/' when absent.

health_path.description: URL path for the primary health endpoint on the staging environment (e.g. '/health'). Used by staging sub-agents to determine if the environment is up before running further checks. Default: '/health' when absent.

commands.description: Shell commands for each workflow step. All keys are optional; omitted keys fall back to stack defaults or skip with a warning.

test.description: Full test suite command (e.g. 'make test', 'cargo test', 'npm test')

format.description: Auto-formatter command — modifies files in place

format_check.description: Formatting check command — fails if files need reformatting, does not modify files

validate.description: Full validation gate command — typically runs lint + format-check + tests together

test_unit.description: Unit tests only — faster feedback loop, no integration or E2E

test_e2e.description: End-to-end test command — typically slower, may require external services

test_visual.description: Visual regression test command — compares screenshots against baselines

env_check_app.description: Project-provided command for app-specific environment checks (e.g., make env-check-app). When absent, project-specific checks are skipped with a warning. Used by: check-local-env.sh. Callback contract: exit 0 = all project-specific checks passed; exit non-zero = one or more checks failed (check-local-env.sh records the failure and exits 1). The command is invoked via eval in the calling script's environment; no additional environment variables are injected by the plugin beyond what is already exported in the shell (APP_PORT, DB_PORT, DB_CONTAINER are available if set). The callback is responsible for its own output — check-local-env.sh does not suppress or capture stdout/stderr.

env_check_cmd.description: Full environment check command invoked by agent-batch-lifecycle.sh preflight step 1d. When absent, the env check step is skipped (ENV_CHECK: skipped). Exit 0 = environment healthy; exit non-zero = environment issues found (preflight exits 1). Invoked via eval with stdout and stderr suppressed.

test_changed.description: Command to run changed integration/E2E tests before committing. When absent, Step 1.5 of the commit workflow is skipped. Typically a project-specific script path (e.g., './scripts/run-changed-tests.sh').

jira.description: Jira integration configuration. Used by `tk sync` for bidirectional ticket synchronization.

project.description: Jira project key used by `tk sync`. Env var JIRA_PROJECT takes precedence.

issue_tracker.description: Issue tracker CLI configuration. Used by commit-failure-tracker and validation scripts.

search_cmd.description: Command to search for existing tickets by substring. Used by commit-failure-tracker.sh.

create_cmd.description: Command to create a new tracking issue. Used by check-validation-failures.sh.

design.description: Design system configuration. Parameterizes design-related skills (design-onboarding, design-review, ui-designer) so they reference the project's design system from config rather than hardcoded values.

system_name.description: Name and version of the design system used by the project (e.g. 'USWDS 3.x', 'Material UI 5', 'None (custom)').

component_library.description: Component library identifier used for adapter selection in ui-designer and component lookup (e.g. 'uswds', 'material', 'bootstrap').

template_engine.description: Template engine used for rendering UI components. Used by ui-designer for adapter selection (e.g. 'jinja2', 'react', 'vue').

design_notes_path.description: Path to the project's North Star design document, relative to the repo root. Referenced by design-review and design-onboarding skills.

manifest_patterns.description: Glob patterns for design manifest files (relative to repo root). Used by verify-baseline-intent.sh to locate design manifests for baseline intent checks. Defaults to ['designs/*/manifest.md', 'designs/*/brief.md'] when absent.

visual.description: Visual regression configuration. Used by verify-baseline-intent.sh for baseline intent checks.

baseline_directory.description: Path to the visual baseline snapshots directory (relative to repo root). Used by verify-baseline-intent.sh for its baseline intent check. Note: this differs from merge.visual_baseline_path, which is used by merge-to-main.sh for the diff check.

database.description: Database lifecycle configuration. Used by agent-batch-lifecycle.sh for DB preflight, status checks, and port resolution. All keys are optional — when absent, the corresponding lifecycle step emits a warning and succeeds as a no-op.

ensure_cmd.description: Command to start the database (e.g. 'make db-start', 'docker compose up -d db'). Used by preflight --start-db.

status_cmd.description: Command to check database status. Exit 0 = running, non-zero = stopped. Used by pre-check --db and preflight.

port_cmd.description: Command to resolve the database port for the current worktree. Receives worktree name as $1 and port type as $2. Used by preflight for port conflict detection.

base_port.description: Base database port number. Worktree-specific ports are derived by adding an offset to this value. Defaults to 5432 when absent.

worktree.description: Worktree lifecycle configuration. Used by worktree-create.sh and claude-safe for pre-launch and post-create steps.

post_create_cmd.description: Command to run after creating a new worktree (relative to repo root). Used by worktree-create.sh post-create phase. When absent, post-create phase is skipped.

service_start_cmd.description: Command to run before launching Claude Code (e.g., start background services). Will be used by .claude/scripts/dso claude-safe (pre-launch phase) once wiring is complete (see epic boak). When absent, claude-safe skips the service startup step.

python_version.description: Python version for worktree environment setup (e.g., '3.13', '3.12'). Used by worktree-setup-env.sh to find the correct Python binary via try_find_python. When absent, falls back to any python3 on PATH.

branch_pattern.description: Git branch naming pattern for worktree validation and cleanup. Used to identify branches created by worktree workflows.

max_age_hours.description: Maximum age in hours for automatic worktree cleanup. Worktrees older than this are candidates for removal. Overridden by AGE_HOURS env var.

infrastructure.description: Container infrastructure configuration. Used by agent-batch-lifecycle.sh for stale container cleanup and by check-local-env.sh for environment verification. All keys are optional — when absent, the corresponding check falls back to defaults or is skipped.

container_prefix.description: Docker container name prefix for worktree-specific containers. Used to discover containers belonging to deleted worktrees.

compose_project.description: Docker Compose project name prefix for worktree-specific stacks. The worktree directory name is appended.

db_container.description: Exact Docker container name to look up for the database. Used by check-local-env.sh for container health checks. No default — when absent, DB container check is skipped.

db_container_patterns.description: List of partial Docker container name patterns to match when the exact db_container name is not found. Checked in order; first match wins. Used by check-local-env.sh.

required_tools.description: List of CLI tools that must be present in PATH. check-local-env.sh fails if any are missing. Defaults to [jq, git, curl] when absent.

optional_tools.description: List of CLI tools that are helpful but not required. check-local-env.sh emits a warning (not a failure) if any are missing. Defaults to [shasum] when absent.

db_port.description: Port to connect to for the database health check. Overrides the DB_PORT environment variable. Defaults to 5432 when absent.

app_port.description: Port to connect to for the application health check. Overrides the APP_PORT environment variable. Defaults to 3000 when absent.

health_timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP health checks performed by check-local-env.sh. Defaults to 5 when absent.

app_base_port.description: Base application port number. Worktree-specific ports are derived by adding an offset to this value. Defaults to 3000 when absent.

compose_files.description: Docker Compose files to shut down on session exit. Will be used by .claude/scripts/dso claude-safe (post-exit Docker cleanup) once wiring is complete (see epic boak). When absent or Docker not on PATH, the cleanup step is skipped silently.

compose_db_file.description: Docker Compose file specifically for database services. Used by worktree cleanup to tear down database containers. When absent but compose_project or container_prefix is set, a partial-config warning is emitted.

session.description: Session management configuration. Used by agent-batch-lifecycle.sh for context window usage checks. All keys are optional — when absent, usage checks are skipped.

usage_check_cmd.description: Command to check session context window usage. Exit 0 = usage IS high (>90%), non-zero = normal. Used by pre-check and context-check subcommands.

artifact_prefix.description: Prefix for /tmp artifact directories (e.g. lockpick-test-artifacts). When absent, derived from basename of git repo root + '-test-artifacts'.

checks.description: Optional quality check commands. When a key is absent, the corresponding check is skipped.

script_write_scan_dir.description: Directory to scan for coupling-lint violations. When absent, the script-writes check is skipped.

assertion_density_cmd.description: Command to run assertion density analysis on test files. When absent, assertion_coverage is scored null in retro reviews.

review.description: Review system configuration. Controls review resolution loop budget and complexity classifier behavior.

max_resolution_attempts.description: Maximum autonomous fix/defend attempts before escalating to the user. Controls the Autonomous Resolution Loop in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, test-failure delegation in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md and TEST-FAILURE-DISPATCH.md.

behavioral_patterns.description: Semicolon-delimited glob list of file patterns the review complexity classifier treats as behavioral (full scoring weight). Files matching these patterns receive higher blast_radius and critical_path scores.

merge.description: Merge-to-main configuration. Controls config-driven behavior of merge-to-main.sh.

visual_baseline_path.description: Path to visual baseline snapshot directory (relative to repo root). When absent, merge-to-main.sh skips the baseline intent check.

ci_workflow_name.description: GitHub Actions workflow name for gh workflow run. When absent, post-push CI trigger recovery is skipped.

message_exclusion_pattern.description: Regex pattern for filtering commits when composing the merge message. Defaults to ^chore: post-merge cleanup.

persistence.description: Persistence coverage validation. Used by check-persistence-coverage.sh to verify data-layer code has corresponding integration tests.

source_patterns.description: Literal substring patterns (grep -F) for identifying persistence/data-layer source files. Repeatable key in flat config.

test_patterns.description: Extended regex patterns (grep -E) for identifying persistence integration test files. Paired with source_patterns.

skills.description: Skill-specific configuration namespace. Each key is consumed by a plugin skill at runtime. All keys are optional — when absent, the skill falls back to generic inline guidance. Values that are file paths are relative to the repo root.

playwright_debug_reference.description: Path to the project-specific playwright-debug reference file, relative to the repo root. Read by the /dso:playwright-debug skill for framework-specific symptom tables, code patterns, and worked examples. When omitted, the skill uses generic inline fallback guidance.

tickets.description: Ticket management configuration. Parameterizes the tk script and ticket workflows, decoupling ID prefixes, directory paths, and sync settings from hardcoded values.

prefix.description: Ticket ID prefix used when generating new ticket IDs. When omitted, the tk wrapper derives the prefix from the project directory name.

directory.description: Directory where ticket markdown files are stored, relative to the repo root.

sync.description: Ticket sync configuration for Jira integration.

jira_project_key.description: Jira project key for ticket sync. Optional — only needed when using tk sync with Jira.

bidirectional_comments.description: Enable bidirectional comment sync between local tickets and Jira. When true, comments added locally are pushed to Jira and vice versa.

orchestration.description: Multi-agent orchestration configuration. Controls the usage-aware throttling pipeline in agent-batch-lifecycle.sh. All keys are optional — when absent, dispatch is uncapped and throttling thresholds apply only from check-usage.sh exit codes.

max_agents.description: Upper bound on the number of concurrent sub-agents dispatched per batch. When null or absent, no config-level cap is applied and the effective MAX_AGENTS is determined solely by check-usage.sh throttle tier (unlimited/throttled/paused). When set, _compute_max_agents() in agent-batch-lifecycle.sh uses min(max_agents, throttle_tier_limit) as the effective cap. Set to 1 to serialize all dispatch regardless of usage tier.

preplanning.description: Preplanning skill configuration. Controls runtime behaviour of the /dso:preplanning skill.

interactive.description: When true, preplanning pauses for user confirmation at interactive gates. When false, all confirmation gates are bypassed (non-interactive / automation mode). When absent, the skill defaults to true (interactive). Set to false in CI pipelines and automated orchestration.
